title: OS Open Greenspace
short_description: Locations and polygon boundaries of all public play spaces
category: playing fields and sports facilities across Great Britain.
provider: OS Open Greenspace contains polygon boundaries and access point locations for all publicly accessible recreational and leisure spaces in Great Britain, including play spaces, public parks, playing fields, golf courses, bowling greens, allotments and tennis courts. Data covers all urban and rural areas of England, Scotland and Wales, updated every six months. The Play Space typology directly captures children's play areas as polygon features with site name and area. Widely used by local authorities, planners, developers and public health analysts to assess and map green space provision.
date_coverage: GeoPackage, GML, Shapefile, Vector Tile; GeoJSON via OS Features API
sample_rows: Free bulk download — registration required via OS Data Hub (osdatahub.os.uk)
tags: id|string|Unique TOID identifier for the feature; distinctive_name_1|string|Primary name of the greenspace site; primary_function|string|Type of greenspace (Play Space / Public Park or Garden / Playing Field / Bowling Green / Tennis Court / Golf Course / Other Sports Facility / Allotments or Community Growing Spaces); secondary_function|string|Secondary function where applicable; area_ha|decimal|Area of the site in hectares; geometry|polygon|Polygon boundary of the site in EPSG:27700 (British National Grid)
access_type: UNKNOWN — requires OS Data Hub account registration to access raw download
priority: playgrounds, play spaces, parks, greenspace, ordnance survey, GIS, great britain, shapefile, open data, leisure
column22: Includes a separate access_point layer showing entry/exit coordinates for each site — directly useful for routing apps. Play Space typology is the key filter for SafeZone Labs use case. Does not include on-street road sign locations (Diagram 545) — only the physical extent of play areas. OS MasterMap Greenspace Layer (commercial) provides greater attribute detail. All 7 greenspace typologies: Play Space; Public Park or Garden; Playing Field; Golf Course; Bowling Green; Tennis Court; Other Sports Facility; Allotments or Community Growing Spaces.

title: ONS Number of Parks and Play Areas in Local Areas England and Wales
short_description: Statistical counts of parks and play areas by local authority district and built-up area in England and Wales.
long_description: Published as part of the ONS Access to Local Amenities bulletin (October 2024), this dataset counts the number of parks and play areas in every local authority district, built-up area and combined authority in England and Wales. Source data is derived from Ordnance Survey's National Geographic Database (NGD) API. Released as aggregated statistical tables rather than geocoded point data, making it suitable for gap analysis and deprivation modelling but not for map-based routing. Coverage is England and Wales only; Scotland and Northern Ireland are excluded.
record_count: One row per local area geography
date_coverage: 2024 (latest release; part of October 2024 Access to Local Amenities publication)
access_method: Free bulk download — no registration required
schema_fields: area_code|string|ONS area code for the geography (e.g. E06000001); area_name|string|Name of the local area; geography_type|string|Type of geography (LAD / BUA / Combined Authority); number_of_parks|integer|Count of public parks in the area; number_of_play_areas|integer|Count of children's play areas in the area
sample_rows: UNKNOWN — requires download from ONS page
tags: play areas, parks, local authority, england, wales, ons, statistics, deprivation, greenspace, access to amenities
notes: Statistical counts only — no individual site coordinates. Useful for sufficiency analysis and identifying under-served areas. Underlying data is OS NGD which is the more granular source. Does not cover Scotland or Northern Ireland.

title: Playgrounds — Barrow Borough Council
short_description: Point locations of council-maintained playgrounds within Barrow Borough including equipment and inspection details.
long_description: A spatial point dataset of playground locations maintained by Barrow Borough Council (Cumbria), published on data.gov.uk as open data. Each playground is recorded as a point feature. Representative of the type of borough-level playground dataset published by individual UK councils. Includes location coordinates and likely inspection or equipment metadata. Coverage is limited to the Barrow-in-Furness local authority area. Useful as a building block for a national aggregated playground dataset.
schema_fields: Free bulk download — no registration required
sample_rows: id|string|Unique identifier for the playground; name|string|Name or reference of the playground; lat|decimal|Latitude coordinate; lon|decimal|Longitude coordinate; inspection_date|date|Date of last inspection
tags: UNKNOWN — data.gov.uk domain inaccessible during research; fields inferred from similar council datasets and should be verified directly
access_type: playgrounds, play areas, cumbria, barrow, council, GeoJSON, point data, inspection, children, leisure
column21: Domain data.gov.uk was inaccessible during this research session. Recommend verifying publisher, coverage, licence, schema and sample data directly on data.gov.uk. Representative of ~30+ similar council-level playground datasets published on data.gov.uk — see also: Children's Play Area (data.gov.uk ID 6250bfcc) and Play Areas CCGBC (data.gov.uk ID 210d75c4). A national aggregation of all such council datasets would be highly valuable.

This is one of several council-level play area datasets on the national open data portal. Likely contains point or polygon geometries with facility name, location and condition details. Coverage is expected to be limited to one local authority area.

title: Play Areas — Caerphilly County Borough Council
short_description: Locations and boundaries of council-maintained play areas across Caerphilly County Borough in South Wales.
long_description: A spatial dataset of play areas maintained by Caerphilly County Borough Council (CCGBC), published on data.gov.uk. Covers the Caerphilly local authority area in South Wales. Each record represents one play area. Representative of Welsh council open data on playground assets. Full schema, record count and format details were not confirmable during this session as the data.gov.uk domain was inaccessible. Useful as one component of a national play area aggregation.

title: London School Streets Schemes
short_description: Locations of 500+ temporary road closure schemes near London schools during drop-off and pick-up times funded by TfL.
long_description: Over 500 School Streets schemes have been delivered across London's 33 boroughs with TfL funding since April 2020. Each scheme restricts motor traffic in the road directly in front of a school during morning and afternoon school run periods. The TfL consultation platform maintains a master list by school name and borough, updated as new schemes are approved. Relevant to SafeZone Labs as School Streets define the start and end of a road zone near a child-safety location. No single centralised GeoJSON download is publicly confirmed — data currently exists as a text list on TfL's platform and fragmented across 33 borough websites.
licence: UNKNOWN — check TfL open data terms at tfl.gov.uk/info-for/open-data-users
format: HTML list / PDF (no centralised GeoJSON confirmed)
access_method: Free — browse TfL consultation platform; borough-level lists on individual council websites
schema_fields: school_name|string|Name of the school with the scheme; street_address|string|Road name affected by closure; borough|string|London borough; operational_hours|string|Times when restriction is in force; restriction_type|string|Type of closure (modal filter / camera enforcement / physical barrier)
sample_rows: UNKNOWN — no bulk download confirmed; data exists as paginated web list
tags: school streets, road closures, children, safety, london, tfl, transport, schools, active travel, healthy streets
notes: FORMAT: No centralised GeoJSON or CSV download confirmed. High-priority gap for OpenData.London — aggregating and geocoding all 500+ schemes into a single spatial dataset would be uniquely valuable. Individual borough sources include: Richmond (richmond.gov.uk/school_streets_locations), Barking & Dagenham (lbbd.gov.uk/school-streets), Hounslow (hounslow.gov.uk/transport-traffic/school-streets/3), Ealing. A harmonised research dataset was produced for a 2022 academic study (PMC9373876) but is not published as open data. TfL evaluation report PDF at content.tfl.gov.uk/school-streets-evaluation-report-website.pdf contains borough-level summaries. Licence negotiation with TfL likely required for bulk use.

title: TfL GIS Open Data Hub
short_description: Transport for London's open geospatial datasets covering road network
long_description: transport infrastructure and pedestrian safety across London.
category: The TfL ArcGIS Open Data Hub is a portal providing free geospatial datasets from Transport for London covering the capital's road and transport network. Datasets include bus stops, cycle routes, pedestrian crossings, borough boundaries, road speeds and safety interventions. Relevant to SafeZone Labs as it may contain road-level safety zone data and crossing locations near schools. Update schedules vary by layer. Accessible via direct shapefile/GeoJSON download or REST API without registration.
record_count: GeoJSON, Shapefile, CSV, REST API (ArcGIS Feature Service)
schema_fields: Free download or REST API — no registration required for most layers; API key optional
sample_rows: layer_name|string|Name of the TfL GIS layer; feature_id|string|Unique feature identifier; geometry|point or polygon|Spatial geometry; attributes|various|Attributes vary by dataset layer
access_type: transport, GIS, london, roads, infrastructure, tfl, geospatial, open data, safety, pedestrian
column21: Acts as a hub for multiple distinct datasets. As of research date, a dedicated School Streets or playground zone GIS layer was not confirmed in the hub catalogue — recommend browsing gis-tfl.opendata.arcgis.com directly to check current catalogue. ArcGIS domain was blocked during this session. Pedestrian crossing and road safety datasets within the hub may be relevant to proximity analysis around playground zones.

title: OpenStreetMap Extract — London (via London Datastore)
short_description: OpenStreetMap data extract for Greater London including playground locations tagged leisure=playground.
long_description: The London Datastore provides a regularly updated extract of OpenStreetMap data for Greater London. OSM contributors map children's play areas using the tag leisure=playground, capturing location, name and equipment details where volunteered. Coverage is community-contributed and generally comprehensive in London but may be incomplete in some outer-borough areas. The extract also contains school locations and road features useful for proximity and zone analysis. OSM data for the full UK is available from Geofabrik (download.geofabrik.de/europe/great-britain.html) as daily extracts. Data available from 2004 to present.
record_count: Thousands of playground features across London
access_method: Free bulk download — no registration required
schema_fields: osm_id|integer|OpenStreetMap feature ID; name|string|Name of playground if tagged by contributors; leisure|string|Feature type tag (playground); access|string|Access restrictions (public / private / permissive); operator|string|Operator of the facility if known; geometry|point or polygon|Location point or polygon boundary of the playground
sample_rows: UNKNOWN — requires download of extract file to access raw rows
tags: openstreetmap, playground, leisure, london, community data, open database, children, play areas, GIS, GeoPackage
notes: Community-sourced data — completeness varies by area. On-street playground road warning signs (UK Diagram 545) are not consistently mapped in OSM. For full UK coverage use Geofabrik extract at download.geofabrik.de/europe/great-britain.html (daily updates, free, ODbL licence). ODbL share-alike terms require attribution and derived datasets to be released under the same licence — review with legal before commercial use.
